@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the OM651 2.1 CDI

mainEntity.name: How much does an OM651 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM651 typically costs £2,800–£4,800 supply only [MB-QUOTE], varying by power variant and whether ancillaries are included. Fitting adds £520–£1,100 depending on labour rates and vehicle model.

mainEntity.name: Is the OM651 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651 rates 4.0★ out of 5 based on 1,250 UK enquiries and specialist reports [MB-VERIFIED]. It is generally robust, but injector seal and timing chain issues are well known and require proactive maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common OM651 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injector seal failure (the “Black Death” – carbon buildup around the injector) is the most frequently reported issue, typically from 80,000–150,000 miles. Timing chain tensioner wear also occurs, causing cold-start rattle.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the OM651?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651 powers C‑Class, E‑Class, GLC, GLA, GLK, Sprinter, Vito, V‑Class, and some S‑Class and ML‑Class variants – covering cars and vans from 2008 to 2020.

mainEntity.name: How long does the OM651 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular oil changes and timely injector seal replacements, the OM651 routinely exceeds 200,000 miles. Many specialist examples are reported at 250,000+ with no major internal work [MB-INTERVIEW].

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM651 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM651 is Mercedes' most common modern diesel four — dependable, but the 'Black Death' injector seal issue is well documented. Backed by 1,250+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 92% confidence — based on 1,250 UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of cheap fixes (injector seals) and labour-intensive work (timing chain))
20 (Parts cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers; high-volume engine)
4 (Recon cost as % of lowest host value (~£4k) = 95% → over 75% = 4)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The OM651 is a strong, economical diesel four — the 'Black Death' injector seal issue is well-documented but preventable — insist on full service history.
verdictRating.bestFor: High-mileage drivers who service on schedule.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented injector seal or chain history above 80,000 miles.

rows.variantBadge: C200 CDI, C220 CDI, C250 CDI
E200 CDI, E220 CDI, E250 CDI

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM651 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [MB-QUOTE].
